toc.text: I. Common Troubleshooting and Maintenance Manual for the IAS-5100
II. Step-by-Step Removal and Installation of the IAS-5100
IV Specific Maintenance Solutions for Various Issues

details.text: 1.1 When the instrument is returned for repair, unpack and reproduce the client-side fault.

steps.title: Check the Kernel Version of the Instrument

details.text: 2.1 Navigate to System Settings - Advanced Settings - enter the password admin123 - OK - access the system backend, and click on Detailed Information in the upper left of Device Information to check the kernel version.

steps.title: Troubleshoot Issues by Referring to the Common Troubleshooting Manual Based on the Kernel Version

troubleshooting.title: IAS-5100 Common Troubleshooting and Maintenance Manual

items.inspection: Check if the power outlet has electricity and if the adapter outputs 24V

items.possibleCause: Damaged or loose 40-pin gray ribbon cable
items.inspection: Check for damage or poor connection of the 40-pin cable
Measure the rocker switch with a multimeter for continuity. Replace if not conducting.
Check if the peripheral board LED lights up after powering on. Replace if not.
Replace the ARM board
items.maintenance: Replace the ARM board and manually input coefficients

items.inspection: Measure the rocker switch with a multimeter for continuity. Replace if not conducting.
Replace the main control board
Replace the screen

items.category: Stuck on Penguin Interface During Boot
items.inspection: Replace the main control board

items.category: Stuck on Penguin Interface During Boot
items.inspection: Replace the ARM board and manually input coefficients
items.maintenance: Replace the ARM board and manually input coefficients

items.inspection: Reconnect the XHB cable, apply adhesive, or replace it
items.maintenance: Reconnect the XHB cable, apply adhesive, or replace it

items.possibleCause: Damaged or loose 40-pin gray ribbon cable
items.inspection: Check for damage or poor connection of the 40-pin cable
Visually inspect the 485 communication cable for looseness
Visually inspect the analyzer USB interface for looseness or soldering issues. If such issues are found, replace the analyze mainboard.
Reconnect the USB cable. If the issue persists, replace the USB cable.

items.possibleCause: Malfunctioning USB interface on the peripheral board
items.inspection: Insert a USB drive and check if the USB icon appears. If it does not, replace the peripheral board.
Observe whether the lamp illuminates normally during startup. If it does not, replace the lamp.

items.possibleCause: Damaged or loose 40-pin gray ribbon cable
items.inspection: Check for damage or poor connection of the 40-pin cable
Use a multimeter to measure the output voltage (should be ~11V). If not, replace the lamp board.
During startup, manually rotate the rotary disc and check for torque. If none, replace the peripheral board.
Replace the PD board
Replace the analyzer mainboard
Replace the DMD board
Replace the ARM board and manually input coefficients
items.maintenance: Replace the ARM board and manually input coefficients

items.inspection: Block strong light and check if the fault clears. If not, replace the sensor.

items.possibleCause: Damaged or loose 40-pin gray ribbon cable
items.inspection: Check for damage or poor connection of the 40-pin cable
Reassemble or replace the rotary disc
items.maintenance: Reassemble or replace the rotary disc

items.inspection: During startup, manually rotate the rotary disc and check for torque. If none, replace the peripheral board.
During startup, manually rotate the rotary disc and check for torque. If none, replace the motor board.
Replace the Motor
Observe whether the lamp illuminates normally during startup. If it does not, replace the lamp.
Use a multimeter to measure the output voltage (should be ~11V). If not, replace the lamp board.
Replace the DMD board
Replace the PD board
Replace the analyzer core board
Replace the optical fiber
Perform self-check after a 15-minute warm-up.
Replace the lamp or peripheral board

steps.instruction: Remove the three black Phillips countersunk screws within the circle and take off the rotary disc.
Remove the four black Phillips umbrella head screws within the marked area.
After removing the battery cover, unscrew the 11 screws on the back panel.
Unscrew the 7 Phillips self-tapping screws on the base.
Remove the white adhesive from the FPC ribbon cable and disconnect the cable from the peripheral board.
Follow the reverse sequence of the above steps for reassembly.
Remove the three black Phillips countersunk screws within the circle and take off the rotary disc.
Remove the four black Phillips umbrella head screws within the marked area.
After removing the battery cover, unscrew the 11 screws on the back panel.
Unscrew the 7 Phillips self-tapping screws on the base.
Disconnect the network cable and the spectrometer's 485 communication cable, then remove the Enclosure.
Follow the reverse sequence of the above steps for reassembly.
Remove the 11 screws from the back panel.
Press the clips on the black switch connector and pull upward to unplug it.
Reach behind the switch, press the clips on both sides, and push the switch outward.
Use scissors to cut the zip tie securing the switch. Remove the old switch and insert the new switch (reverse these steps for reassembly).
Remove the enclosure
steps.imageNote: Refer to IAS-5100 Removal and Installation 1

reference.label: Refer to IAS-5100 Removal and Installation 1

steps.instruction: Disconnect all ribbon cables connected to the peripheral board, remove the four screws around it, and take out the peripheral board.
Install the new peripheral board, secure it with the four screws, reconnect the previously removed ribbon cables to the new board, and reassemble the machine in reverse order.
Remove the enclosure
steps.imageNote: Refer to IAS-5100 Removal and Installation 2

reference.label: Refer to IAS-5100 Removal and Installation 2

steps.instruction: Disconnect all ribbon cables connected to the peripheral board, remove the four screws around it, and take out the peripheral board.
After removing the peripheral board, remove the motor board on the peripheral board
Install the removed motor board to the new peripheral board
Follow the reverse sequence of the above steps for reassembly.
Remove the enclosure
steps.imageNote: Refer to IAS-5100 Removal and Installation 1

reference.label: Refer to IAS-5100 Removal and Installation 1

steps.instruction: After removing the enclosure, disconnect the white glue fixed in the circle and remove the blue ARM board in the center of the main control board.
Insert the new ARM board after removal, and reassemble the machine in reverse order.
Remove the enclosure
steps.imageNote: Refer to IAS-5100 Removal and Installation 2

reference.label: Refer to IAS-5100 Removal and Installation 2

steps.instruction: After removing the enclosure, remove the blue ARM board in the center of the main control board
Insert the new ARM board after removal, and reassemble the machine in reverse order.
Remove the enclosure
steps.imageNote: Refer to IAS-5100 Removal and Installation

reference.label: Refer to IAS-5100 Removal and Installation

steps.instruction: Remove the tape on the screen cable and disconnect the cable
After removing the screws in the circle, take out the right pivot
Take out the screen from the front
Reinstall the screen from the front (kernel versions starting from 1.X require the installation of KEDACOM screen; 2.X or 4.X require the installation of GRAHOWLET screen)

Insert the pivot con the right side as shown in the diagram. Place the pivot bracket onto both pivots, and secure the pivot bracket with two M3*8 Phillips self-tapping screws on each side. Ensure the screen does not wobble by checking for front-to-back stability on the right side.

Secure the main control board bracket using four M3*8 Phillips self-tapping screws. Ensure the protruding side is on the left, and add two spacers to each of the four rivet studs on the main control board bracket.
Use four M3*8 Phillips combination screws to reinstall the control board. Connect the screen ribbon cable, apply 3M adhesive to the cable, and plug in the collection button cable.
After installing the screen, reassemble the machine in reverse order of disassembly.
Remove the enclosure
steps.imageNote: Refer to IAS-5100 Removal and Installation 1

reference.label: Refer to IAS-5100 Removal and Installation 1

steps.instruction: After removing the enclosure, disconnect the white adhesive securing the ribbon cables to the control board and unplug all ribbon cables connected to the control board.
Remove the central blue ARM board from the removed control motherboard
Install the removed blue ARM board on the new control main board
Follow the reverse sequence of the above steps for reassembly.
Remove the enclosure
steps.imageNote: Refer to IAS-5100 Removal and Installation 2

reference.label: Refer to IAS-5100 Removal and Installation 2

steps.instruction: After removing the enclosure, disconnect the cable connected to the main control board and remove the main control board
Remove the blue ARM board in the center and insert it into the new control board
Follow the reverse sequence of the above steps for reassembly.

documents.title: Replace the Circuit Board of the NIR Analyzer
documents.sourceSheet: Replace the Circuit Board of th

steps.instruction: Remove the enclosure
steps.imageNote: Refer to IAS-5100 Removal and Installation

reference.label: Refer to IAS-5100 Removal and Installation

steps.instruction: After removing the enclosure, the position of the spectrometer is shown in the figure.

As shown in the figure, first remove the two Phillips screws in the red circle that secure the DMD circuit board. Then remove the DMD board, and finally disconnect the ribbon cables connected to the DMD board (the ribbon cables are secured in a drawer-style manner; the sides can be gently pulled outward to remove the cables). When installing a new DMD board, reassemble in reverse order of disassembly.
As shown in the figure, use a utility knife to cut the white adhesive connecting the spectrometer.
First, unscrew the two screws securing the optical fiber to the spectrometer. Then, unscrew the two screws securing the spectrometer bracket to the base plate.
Disconnect the USB interface connected to the core board and remove the spectrometer

As shown in the diagram, first remove the ribbon cables from the core board and PD board (ensure that the blue side of the ribbon cables is facing outward). Then, remove the screws securing the PD board and the core board, and replace the PD board and core board. When reinstalling, reassemble the PD board and core board in reverse order.

left: 2. Two screws for the PD board
3. Four screws for the core board
right: 4. Remove the wiring of the core board

steps.instruction: Follow the reverse sequence of the above steps for reassembly.
Remove the enclosure
steps.imageNote: Refer to IAS-5100 Removal and Installation 2

reference.label: Refer to IAS-5100 Removal and Installation 2

steps.instruction: Disconnect the 2 cables on the lamp board, remove the 4 fixing screws, and replace with a new light source board.
Follow the reverse sequence of the above steps for reassembly.
Remove the enclosure
steps.imageNote: Refer to IAS-5100 Removal and Installation

reference.label: Refer to IAS-5100 Removal and Installation

steps.instruction: As shown in the figure, remove the four screws on each side of the lamp holder assembly for replacement, making sure to reconnect the green lamp wires.
Follow the reverse sequence of the above steps for reassembly.
Remove the enclosure
steps.imageNote: Refer to IAS-5100 Removal and Installation 2

reference.label: Refer to IAS-5100 Removal and Installation 2

steps.instruction: Remove the 5 fixing screws on the peripheral board (one of which is a side screw) and take off the peripheral board
Remove the 4 fixing screws on the motor board and replace the motor board with a new one.
Follow the reverse sequence of the above steps for reassembly.
Remove the enclosure
steps.imageNote: Refer to IAS-5100 Removal and Installation

reference.label: Refer to IAS-5100 Removal and Installation

steps.instruction: Remove the motor wire and the four screws fixing the motor, take off the motor, and reinstall it in reverse order (note that the motor wiring port is on the upper left when reinstalling it)
Follow the reverse sequence of the above steps for reassembly.
Remove the enclosure
steps.imageNote: Refer to IAS-5100 Removal and Installation 1

reference.label: Refer to IAS-5100 Removal and Installation 1

steps.instruction: Disconnect the XHB or FC-20 cable and replace it with a new one
After completing the replacement, reassemble the instrument in reverse order.
Remove the enclosure
steps.imageNote: Refer to IAS-5100 Removal and Installation 2

reference.label: Refer to IAS-5100 Removal and Installation 2

steps.instruction: Disconnect the 40-pin gray ribbon cable connected to the control board and replace it with a new cable.
Follow the reverse sequence of the above steps for reassembly.
Remove the enclosure
steps.imageNote: Refer to IAS-5100 Removal and Installation 1

reference.label: Refer to IAS-5100 Removal and Installation 1

steps.instruction: Remove the two fixing screws in the circle
Disconnect the sensor wiring connected to the peripheral board, remove the sensor, and replace it with a new one
Follow the reverse sequence of the above steps for reassembly.
Remove the enclosure
steps.imageNote: Refer to IAS-5100 Removal and Installation 2

reference.label: Refer to IAS-5100 Removal and Installation 2

steps.instruction: Remove the two fixing screws in the circle
Disconnect the sensor wiring connected to the peripheral board, remove the sensor, and replace it with a new one
Follow the reverse sequence of the above steps for reassembly.
Remove the enclosure
steps.imageNote: Refer to IAS-5100 Removal and Installation

reference.label: Refer to IAS-5100 Removal and Installation

steps.instruction: Disconnect the USB cable connected to the peripheral board and spectrometer and replace it with a new one
Follow the reverse sequence of the above steps for reassembly.
Remove the enclosure
steps.imageNote: Refer to IAS-5100 Removal and Installation

reference.label: Refer to IAS-5100 Removal and Installation

steps.instruction: After using needle nose pliers to loosen the fiber, unscrew one end of the fiber by hand
Use a screwdriver to remove the screws securing the other end of the optical fiber to the spectrometer, and then remove the optical fiber.
Follow the reverse sequence of the above steps for reassembly.
Remove the enclosure
steps.imageNote: Refer to IAS-5100 Removal and Installation 2

reference.label: Refer to IAS-5100 Removal and Installation 2

steps.instruction: Disconnect the spectrometer 485 communication cable and replace it with a new cable (ensure the wire sequence is correct).
Follow the reverse sequence of the above steps for reassembly.
